article_title: Realta Fusion Unveils Hammir-DT, a Tandem Magnetic Mirror Power Plant Design Targeting 500 MW and Sub-$50/MWh Electricity
summary: All engineering specifics, cost figures, quotes, and the internal cross-reference verified against source snapshots; only finding is a non-blocking allowlist warning.

content_preview.title: Realta Fusion Unveils Hammir-DT, a Tandem Magnetic Mirror Power Plant Design Targeting 500 MW and Sub-$50/MWh Electricity
content_preview.summary: The Madison startup detailed a 500 MW deuterium-tritium magnetic mirror plant using REBCO magnets from Commonwealth Fusion Systems, claiming a $50/MWh path.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Realta Fusion, the Madison, Wisconsin startup pursuing magnetic mirror fusion, has detailed Hammir-DT, a preconceptual design for a deuterium-tritium power plant it describes as the first modern axisymmetric tandem mirror power plant design. According to [The Fusion Report](https://thefusionreport.com/this-weeks-fusion-news-june-12-2026/), the design targets 500 MW of fusion power, 538 MW thermal, and approximately 200 MWe delivered through direct energy conversion. The company has ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece with Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis sections. Appropriate technical depth on tandem mirror architecture, gain figures, and economics. 874 words, within the News 400-1200 range.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 source snapshots read from disk (gunzipped) and verified. source-0 (thefusionreport.com weekly, status 200): confirms preconceptual D-T tandem mirror, 500 MW fusion / 538 MW thermal / ~200 MWe via direct energy conversion, REBCO magnets from CFS, stackable 100-500 MW per unit, CapEx $1B-$2B, LCOE below $50/MWh, $54M raised to date, 'significant raise' needed. source-1 (thefusionreport.substack, status 200): confirms QSCI ~5.3, QENG 1.3, 538MW thermal with extra 38 MW exothermic energy from breeding tritium from lithium-6, four high-field REBCO magnets per plant (two at each end), central cell 1-3 Tesla, direct energy conversion. source-2 (PRNewswire Realta-CFS partnership, status 200): both quotes verbatim — Furlong ('By working with the world's leading HTS magnet manufacturer, we are significantly de-risking one of the most critical of these technologies') correctly attributed to Realta CEO Kieran Furlong; Mumgaard ('As the world leader in HTS magnet technology development and manufacturing, we are pleased to share our expertise with Realta') correctly attributed to Bob Mumgaard, CFS CEO and Co-founder. Confirms Devens MA, CFS develops HTS for demonstration prototypes and commercial plants, ARPA-E funded UW-Madison to build WHAM in 2020, Realta spun out 2022, WHAM first plasma at world-record 17 tesla in 2024. source-3 (Power Online, status 200): confirms Q>5 with 50-meter center cell and Q>10 or more with longer cell; Frank quote ('Our model proves there's a there there when it comes to CoSMo fusion energy systems based on the tandem mirror') verbatim and correctly attributed to Realta's computational physics team leader Dr. Sam Frank; eight organizations in DOE Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program 2023; $36M Series A led by Future Ventures with Khosla Ventures backing.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every engineering specific (500 MW / 538 MW / ~200 MWe, CapEx $1B-$2B, LCOE <$50/MWh, QSCI 5.3, QENG 1.3, four magnets / 1-3 Tesla central cell, 38 MW lithium-6 breeding), funding figure ($54M to date, $36M Series A), and historical milestone (WHAM 2020 build, 2022 spinout, 17-tesla 2024 record, DOE 2023 program) traces to a cited source. The article correctly frames the design figures as company projections / preconceptual targets — 'preconceptual design', 'stated design goals', 'modeled targets rather than demonstrated results' — not validated results.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately tempers the company's claims (preconceptual, modeled targets, no construction timeline or site, large funding gap). No sensationalism despite the headline figures.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Realta Fusion or magnetic mirror coverage in the archive. Distinct from the June 10 Commonwealth Fusion ARC article, which it correctly cross-references; the internal link /article/2026-06/10-commonwealth-fusion-publishes-five-peer-reviewed-papers-validating-the-physics-of-its-400-mw-arc-power-plant resolves to an existing published article.
recommendations: Three sources (thefusionreport.com, thefusionreport.substack.com, poweronline.com) are not in config/source_allowlist.txt — non-blocking warning. PRNewswire is the primary press release for the partnership/quotes; The Fusion Report carries the exclusive Hammir-DT interview and Power Online carries the modeling milestones. Consider adding these domains to the allowlist if future fusion coverage will rely on them.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, accurate, well-sourced submission. All claims, quotes, and the internal cross-reference verified against source snapshots. The single finding is a non-blocking allowlist warning, which does not require a correction. APPROVE.
